title: procedure-trial-structure
description: JEAB Procedure category, trial-structure sub-annotator. Declares session-level trial composition for free-operant schedules with mixed trial types: peak procedure (Catania & Reynolds, 1968), reinstatement tests (Baker et al., 1991), and free-operant temporal bisection (Fetterman & Killeen, 1995). Complements procedure-stimulus @testing (which applies to discrete-trial MTS only).

trial_mix.description: Session-level composition of a Mix/Mult schedule into trial types with counts, durations, and reinforcement. Applies to free-operant Mix/Mult combinators only. For discrete-trial MTS probe testing, use @testing (procedure-stimulus) instead. The annotation captures paper-level Methods description (counts, ordering specification); runtime randomization details (seed, shuffling algorithm) are delegated to execution_profile.

type.description: Trial-mix variant. 'peak': reinforced FI trials intermixed with extended unreinforced probe trials (Catania & Reynolds, 1968; Roberts, 1981). 'reinstatement': within-session response-independent reinforcer deliveries during/after extinction to induce recovery of responding (Baker et al., 1991; Ostlund & Balleine, 2007). 'temporal_bisection': short/long anchor trials intermixed with intermediate probe durations for psychophysical scaling (Fetterman & Killeen, 1995; free-operant variant of Church & Deluty, 1977).

reinforced_ref.description: Peak only: zero-based index of the reinforced (standard FI) component within the annotated Mix/Mult expression.

reinforced_count.description: Peak only: number of reinforced (standard) trials per session.

probe_ref.description: Peak and temporal_bisection: zero-based index of the probe (unreinforced) component. For peak, this is the extended FI trial; for temporal_bisection, this is the intermediate-duration probe component.

probe_count.description: Peak and temporal_bisection: number of probe trials per session. For temporal_bisection, this is the count per probe duration in probe_duration_list (total probe trials = probe_count * len(probe_duration_list)).

probe_duration.description: Peak only: duration of each probe trial (typically 3-4x the reinforced FI value; Catania & Reynolds, 1968).

extinction_ref.description: Reinstatement only: zero-based index of the extinction component (the target operant with reinforcement withheld).

reinstatement_ref.description: Reinstatement only: zero-based index of the response-independent reinforcer delivery component (typically FT; Zeiler, 1968).

reinstatement_count.description: Reinstatement only: number of response-independent reinforcer deliveries (Baker et al., 1991 used 4; varies 1-16 across literature).

reinstatement_interval.description: Reinstatement only, optional: inter-delivery interval for response-independent reinforcers. When omitted, the runtime derives it from the reinstatement_ref component's schedule parameter (e.g., FT60s implies 60s interval).

short_anchor_ref.description: Temporal bisection only: zero-based index of the short-duration anchor component (reinforced; trains 'short' response class).

short_anchor_duration.description: Temporal bisection only: stimulus duration of the short anchor (e.g., 2s in Church & Deluty, 1977).

short_anchor_count.description: Temporal bisection only: number of short anchor trials per session.

long_anchor_ref.description: Temporal bisection only: zero-based index of the long-duration anchor component (reinforced; trains 'long' response class).

long_anchor_duration.description: Temporal bisection only: stimulus duration of the long anchor (e.g., 8s in Church & Deluty, 1977).

long_anchor_count.description: Temporal bisection only: number of long anchor trials per session.

probe_duration_list.description: Temporal bisection only: list of intermediate probe durations as quoted time-value strings (e.g., ["3s", "4s", "5s", "6s", "7s"]). Typically 3-7 values logarithmically or linearly spaced between short_anchor_duration and long_anchor_duration. Validator parses each string as a time_value.

ordering_spec.description: Paper-level ordering specification. 'random': trials randomly interspersed (typical for peak and temporal_bisection). 'blocked': trial types presented in separate blocks. 'fixed_sequence': deterministic paper-specified order (typical for reinstatement: extinction block -> reinforcer deliveries -> test). Runtime implementation details (seed, shuffling algorithm) are delegated to execution_profile, preserving AST/execution orthogonality.

trial_mix.applies_to_note: Applies only to Core Mix/Mult combinators (free-operant response opportunity). Applying to MTS/GoNoGo trial-based primitives triggers TRIAL_MIX_WRONG_PRIMITIVE linter warning; use @testing (procedure-stimulus) for MTS probe testing. This separation reflects Theorem 1 (core-trial-based/theory.md §4): free-operant and discrete-trial paradigms have incompatible semantics. Component count requirements vary by type: peak requires >=2 components, reinstatement requires >=2 components, temporal_bisection requires >=3 components.

errors.TRIAL_MIX_WRONG_PRIMITIVE: @trial_mix applies only to Mix/Mult combinators (free-operant); discrete-trial MTS/GoNoGo should use @testing instead
errors.TRIAL_MIX_INVALID_REF: Component ref (reinforced_ref, probe_ref, extinction_ref, reinstatement_ref, short_anchor_ref, long_anchor_ref) must index a valid component of the Mix/Mult expression
errors.TRIAL_MIX_DUPLICATE_REF: Component refs must reference distinct components within the same @trial_mix annotation
errors.TRIAL_MIX_INSUFFICIENT_COMPONENTS: Mix/Mult expression must have enough components for the declared type (peak: >=2, reinstatement: >=2, temporal_bisection: >=3)
errors.TRIAL_MIX_MISSING_TYPE: type parameter is required; must be one of 'peak', 'reinstatement', 'temporal_bisection'
errors.TRIAL_MIX_FORBIDDEN_PARAM: Parameter not applicable to the declared type (e.g., reinforced_count is peak-only; extinction_ref is reinstatement-only)
errors.TRIAL_MIX_INVALID_PROBE_DURATION: probe_duration_list entries must be parseable as time_value strings (e.g., '3s', '150ms', '1min')
errors.TRIAL_MIX_PROBE_OUT_OF_ANCHOR_RANGE: For temporal_bisection, all probe_duration_list values must fall strictly between short_anchor_duration and long_anchor_duration

trial_mix.reference: Catania, A. C., & Reynolds, G. S. (1968). A quantitative analysis of the responding maintained by interval schedules of reinforcement. Journal of the Experimental Analysis of Behavior, 11(s3), 327-383. https://doi.org/10.1901/jeab.1968.11-s327; Roberts, S. (1981). Isolation of an internal clock. Journal of Experimental Psychology: Animal Behavior Processes, 7(3), 242-268. https://doi.org/10.1037/0097-7403.7.3.242; Eckard, M. L., & Kyonka, E. G. E. (2018). Differential reinforcement of response sequences in peak procedures. Behavioural Processes, 151, 111-118. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.beproc.2018.03.014; Baker, A. G., Steinwald, H., & Bouton, M. E. (1991). Contextual conditioning and reinstatement of extinguished instrumental responding. Quarterly Journal of Experimental Psychology, 43B, 199-218; Ostlund, S. B., & Balleine, B. W. (2007). Selective reinstatement of instrumental performance depends on the discriminative stimulus properties of the mediating outcome. Learning & Behavior, 35, 43-52. https://doi.org/10.3758/BF03196073; Church, R. M., & Deluty, M. Z. (1977). Bisection of temporal intervals. Journal of Experimental Psychology: Animal Behavior Processes, 3(3), 216-228. https://doi.org/10.1037/0097-7403.3.3.216; Fetterman, J. G., & Killeen, P. R. (1995). Categorical scaling of time: Implications for clock-counter models. Journal of Experimental Psychology: Animal Behavior Processes, 21(1), 43-63. https://doi.org/10.1037/0097-7403.21.1.43
